AItitle: Cracking the Credit Spread Enigma: Uncovering the Hidden Role of Bond Market Liquidity
AIsummary: Here is a comprehensive summary of the academic paper in one sentence:This study, situated in the field of finance, investigates the "Global Credit Spread Puzzle" (GCSP), which refers to the persistent underprediction of investment-grade (IG) corporate bond credit spreads by standard default risk models, and proposes a novel solution by incorporating search frictions in the secondary corporate bond market into a reduced-form variant of the He and Milbradt (2014) model.Here is a summary of the paper in 250 words:The Global Credit Spread Puzzle (GCSP) refers to the consistent underprediction of investment-grade (IG) corporate bond credit spreads by standard default risk models. This study aims to solve this puzzle by exploring two potential channels: incorporating mean-reverting leverage ratios and incorporating corporate bond illiquidity.Previous studies have attempted to address the GCSP by modifying the standard default risk model, but these modifications have had limited success. The authors of this study propose a new approach by incorporating search frictions in the secondary corporate bond market into a reduced-form variant of the He and Milbradt (2014) model.The results show that the liquidity component of the model helps capture certain determinants of search frictions and significantly improves overall model performance. Specifically, augmenting the standard default risk model with this liquidity component not only raises average IG spreads but also enhances the model's capability to capture the distribution of credit spreads.Furthermore, the study finds that the GCSP is more pronounced when government bonds are used as the default-free benchmark, rather than swap rates. The authors conclude that integrating elements of corporate debt illiquidity into standard default risk models is essential to better capture IG corporate bond credit spreads, especially spreads over government bonds.Overall, this study contributes to our understanding of the GCSP and proposes a novel solution by incorporating search frictions in the secondary corporate bond market.
AItitle-cn: 破解信用利差之谜：揭示债券市场流动性的隐藏作用
AIsummary-cn: 以下是一句话对学术论文的全面总结：本研究位于金融领域，研究了“全球信用利差难题”（GCSP），即标准违约风险模型对投资级（IG）公司债券信用利差的持续低估，并通过将二级公司债券市场的搜索摩擦纳入He和Milbradt（2014）模型的简化变体中，提出了一种新的解决方案。以下是250字的论文摘要：全球信用利差难题（GCSP）是指标准违约风险模型对投资级（IG）公司债券信用利差的持续低估。本研究旨在通过探索两个潜在渠道来解决这一难题：纳入均值回复杠杆率和纳入公司债券非流动性。之前的研究试图通过修改标准违约风险模型来解决GCSP问题，但这些修改的成功有限。本研究的作者提出了一种新方法，将二级公司债券市场中的搜索摩擦纳入He和Milbradt（2014）模型的简化变体中。结果表明，该模型的流动性成分有助于捕捉搜索摩擦的某些决定因素，并显著提高了整体模型性能。具体而言，用这种流动性成分增强标准违约风险模型不仅提高了平均IG利差，还增强了模型捕捉信用利差分布的能力。此外，研究发现，当政府债券被用作无违约基准而不是掉期利率时，GCSP更为明显。作者得出结论，将公司债务流动性不足的因素纳入标准违约风险模型对于更好地捕捉IG公司债券信用利差，特别是政府债券的利差至关重要。总的来说，这项研究有助于我们理解GCSP，并通过将搜索摩擦纳入二级公司债券市场提出了一种新的解决方案。
Highlights:
学术要点 1：全球信用利差之谜（Global Credit Spread Puzzle, GCSP）|||
结构性模型在校准历史违约数据后，系统性低估跨国投资级债券信用利差的现象，反映传统模型对非违约风险因素的忽略。|||
学术要点 2：内生流动性模型（Endogenous Liquidity Model）|||
通过OTC市场搜索摩擦和议价机制内生化企业债流动性溢价的结构模型，能解构信用利差中的违约与非违约成分。|||
学术要点 3：互换利率基准争议（Swap Rate Benchmark Debate）|||
围绕以LIBOR互换利率替代国债作为"无违约"基准的争论，涉及信用利差中风险溢价与流动性溢价的结构性分离问题。|||
学术要点 4：搜索摩擦参数异质性（Heterogeneous Search Friction Parameters）|||
跨国市场中经销商匹配强度（λ）、流动性冲击强度（ξ）等参数的差异化估计，反映各国二级市场微观结构的制度性差异。|||
学术要点 5：跨国异质性恢复率（Cross-country Heterogeneous Recovery Rates）|||
基于区域划分的动态恢复率估计方法，捕捉主权债务危机等宏观事件对违约回收率的非对称冲击效应。
